proposition_label: Faith is transmitted through apostolic succession
description: The bishops who succeed the apostles are authoritative guarantors of the received tradition.

proposition_label: Baptism effects spiritual regeneration
description: Baptism is not merely symbolic but effects a new birth or regeneration of the recipient.

proposition_label: Baptism is administered with water
description: Baptismal practice uses water as the material element.

proposition_label: Valid Eucharist requires the bishop or his delegate
description: Proper eucharistic celebration is administered by or authorized by the bishop.

proposition_label: A single bishop governs the local church
description: A single bishop holds authority over the local Christian community as representative of God.

proposition_label: Disciples are called to follow Jesus
description: Jesus summons persons into direct discipleship.

proposition_label: The Eucharist is the body and blood of Christ
description: The eucharistic elements are identified as the flesh and blood of Jesus.

proposition_label: The Eucharist is medicine of immortality
description: The eucharistic bread confers immortality and incorruption on those who receive it; sharing in Christ's deathless life.

proposition_label: The Eucharist is a sacrifice offered to God
description: The eucharistic celebration is explicitly described as a sacrifice or oblation offered to God.

proposition_label: Christians gather on Sunday for the Eucharist
description: The Lord's Day is the regular day for eucharistic assembly.

proposition_label: Christians are required to fast
description: Fasting is a required Christian spiritual discipline presented as obligatory, not merely optional.

proposition_label: A final judgment is coming
description: Texts or teachers present a climactic future judgment of the wicked and vindication of the righteous.

proposition_label: Christians should assemble frequently for Eucharist
description: Regular gathering for eucharistic worship is normative.

proposition_label: Gentile believers need not be circumcised
description: Gentile inclusion in the people of God does not require circumcision.

proposition_label: Jesus was truly born of a virgin
description: Jesus was truly conceived by the Holy Spirit and born of the virgin Mary, not through natural human generation.

proposition_label: Jesus is proclaimed as raised
description: The apostolic proclamation announces that Jesus has been raised from the dead.

proposition_label: Jesus is the Christ
description: Texts or teachers explicitly identify Jesus as the Christ or Messiah.

proposition_label: Jesus teaches with unique authority
description: Jesus is portrayed as speaking with authority that stands out from other teachers.

proposition_label: The kingdom of God has drawn near
description: Jesus proclamation presents God kingdom as imminently present or arriving.

proposition_label: Martyrdom is bearing faithful witness to Christ before authorities
description: The martyr's death is presented as a form of witness to Christ, conforming to his passion and declaring undivided fidelity before earthly powers.

proposition_label: The bones of martyrs are precious and gathered for commemoration
description: The bodily remains of martyrs are treated as precious, gathered for custody, and commemorated at annual anniversary gatherings.

proposition_label: Post-baptismal sin can be forgiven through penance
description: A second repentance after baptism is possible and administered through church discipline.

proposition_label: Repentance is required in response to the proclamation
description: The proclamation of the kingdom calls hearers to repentance.

proposition_label: The rule of faith is received from the apostles
description: Christians hold to a received standard of teaching traced to the apostles and guarded by their successors.

proposition_label: Valid church order requires bishop, presbyter, and deacon
description: The threefold ministry of bishop, presbyter, and deacon is required for valid church structure.
